title: view-panel
content:
View Panel

The View panel controls how the scene is displayed, including camera settings, section cutting, helpers, light configuration, multi-object group selection, and appearance preferences.

Basic Controls

  • Fit View – Adjusts the camera so that all loaded models fit fully within the viewport.

  • Show hidden objects – Makes all currently hidden objects visible.

  • Switch hidden objects – Inverts visibility: visible objects become hidden and hidden objects become visible.

  • Wireframe – Toggles wireframe rendering on all meshes. Useful for inspecting geometry without surface shading.

  • X-ray on select - Toggles the display of the selected object. When the switch is on, objects hidden by other objects are also displayed.

  • Selection box – Sets the coordinate space of the bounding-box highlight shown around a selected object. local aligns the box with the object's own axes; world aligns it with the world axes.

  • Background – Color picker for the scene background color.

  • Persp. camera – Switches between perspective (checked) and orthographic (unchecked) projection.

  • Sharp edges – Draws sharp-edge line overlays on mesh surfaces in the viewport. Useful for inspecting creases and boundaries without changing model geometry.

  • Edge angle threshold – Minimum angle between adjacent faces (1–45°) for an edge to qualify as “sharp” when Sharp edges is enabled.

  • Outliner: overlays & tools – When enabled, auxiliary objects appear in the Scene outliner tree: section mesh copies, sharp-edge overlays, and tool objects (distance/angle measurements, dimensions, annotations). Default off keeps the outliner focused on the model hierarchy only; viewport display of these objects is controlled separately (e.g. Sharp edges, Show Section Mesh, Tools panel).

Section View

Cuts the model with clipping planes so the interior can be inspected. Use Mode to choose a three-plane corner cut or a single plane.

  • Section – Enables/disables the section clipping planes and the section gizmo.

  • Mode – Section cut layout: Corner (three planes), Single XY, Single XZ, or Single YZ. In single-plane modes, rotation sliders and gizmo rotate mode are available.

  • Cross Section Lines – Draws colored lines where the cutting plane intersects the mesh surfaces.

  • Cross Lines Color – Color of the cross-section intersection lines.

  • Apply to hidden – When enabled, cross-section lines are also computed for hidden objects.

  • Solid Section – Fills the cut face with a solid cap so the model appears closed. Automatically activates Section if not already on.

  • Cap Color – Fill color of the solid section cap.

  • Show Section Mesh – Toggles a viewport-only mesh copy that makes the cut surface appear filled/solid (back-face rendering with clipping). This is separate from Solid Section cap geometry. Does not affect the Scene outliner unless Outliner: overlays & tools is on.

  • Gizmo – Shows/hides the interactive 3D gizmo that lets you drag the clipping plane position directly in the viewport.

  • Gizmo mode – translate or rotate for the section gizmo (single-plane modes).

  • Snap translation – Snap step (in scene units, 0.1–100) when dragging the section gizmo with Ctrl held.

  • Snap rotation – Snap step in degrees (1–90) when rotating the section gizmo with Ctrl held.

  • Pos. X / Y / Z – Numeric sliders for positioning each of the three clipping planes along their respective axes.

  • Rot. X / Y / Z – Rotation of the clipping plane(s) in single-plane modes (−180° to 180°).

  • Reset section – Resets the clipping planes to the default position around the model's bounding box.

Cross Section Lines (sub-folder)

A secondary cross-section visualisation that draws lines on a single chosen plane, independently of the main clipping planes.

  • Show Lines – Enables the cross-section line overlay.

  • Apply to hidden – Include hidden objects when computing the lines.

  • Update Section Lines – When enabled, the lines are recomputed automatically each time the scene changes.

  • Plane – The world-aligned plane used for the cross-section: XY, XZ, or YZ.

  • Position – Position of the selected plane along its normal axis.

  • Color – Color of the cross-section lines.

Helpers

Toggles for debug/orientation visual aids.

  • axes – Shows the world-space axes helper (RGB arrows = X/Y/Z).

  • axes size – Length of the axes arrows in scene units (1–2000).

  • camera persp – Shows a wireframe frustum visualising the perspective camera.

  • camera ortho – Shows a wireframe box visualising the orthographic camera.

  • lights – Shows helper icons for all scene lights.

  • lights size – Size of the light helper icons in scene units (1–5000).

  • raycast – Shows an arrow helper at the last raycast hit point (useful for debugging picking).

  • raycast size – Length of the raycast arrow in scene units (100–100 000).

Lights

Dynamically generated folder listing all lights currently in the scene. Each light exposes its own controls (color, intensity, position, etc.). The folder title shows the total light count.

Group Selection

Manages a set of simultaneously selected objects (multi-selection group).

  • Box select mode – How drag-box selection matches objects: Overlap (any part) — any part inside the box; Fully inside — entire bounding box must be inside; Center inside — object center must be inside.

  • Box padding – Extra padding (0–200 scene units) added around the bounding box when a group is highlighted.

  • Add/remove selected (/) – Adds the currently selected object to the group, or removes it if it is already a member. Keyboard shortcut: /.

  • Clear group – Removes all objects from the current group.

  • Add to history – Saves the current group composition to the Group History for later recall.

Group History

Stores and recalls previously saved group selections.

  • Entry – Read-only label showing the index and name of the currently previewed history entry.

  • ← Previous [7] – Navigate to the previous history entry. Keyboard shortcut: 7.

  • → Next [9] – Navigate to the next history entry. Keyboard shortcut: 9.

  • Restore [8] – Applies the previewed history entry as the active group selection. Keyboard shortcut: 8.

  • Remove from history – Deletes the currently previewed history entry.

Preferences

Persistent settings for toolbar appearance, document labels, and export compression. Measurement, dimension, and annotation defaults are in the Tools panel.

Toolbar Preferences

  • Toolbar font size – Font size of the top toolbar buttons (9–18 px).

  • Toolbar height – Height of the top toolbar (20–50 px).

  • Font size – Font size used inside all GUI panels (9–18 px).

  • Row height – Height of each row in GUI panels (14–44 px).

  • Panel width – Width of the right-side GUI panels (200–500 px).

  • Set to default – Resets all toolbar preferences to their factory defaults.

Document Name

Controls what date information is shown in the document label in the Docs panel.

  • Show last edit date – Displays the date the document was last edited.

  • Show import date – Displays the date the document was imported into the scene.

GLB Draco Compression

Fine-grained settings for Draco geometry compression used during GLB and HTML export.

  • Use custom settings – When unchecked, the Draco encoder uses its built-in defaults. Check this to unlock the controls below.

  • Method – Draco encoding algorithm: Edgebreaker (better compression, mesh-only) or Sequential (broader compatibility).

  • Encode speed – Encoder speed vs. compression ratio trade-off (0 = best compression / slowest, 10 = fastest / largest).

  • Decode speed – Decoder speed vs. data size trade-off (0 = best / smallest, 10 = fastest / largest).

  • Quantize position / normal / color / texcoord / generic (bits) – Number of quantization bits for each attribute type (1–16). Fewer bits = smaller file but lower precision.

  • Set to default – Resets all compression settings to factory defaults.

Attachments Compression

Lossless gzip compression for file attachments embedded in the GLB.

  • Compress attachments in GLB – When enabled, attachments are gzip-compressed before being stored in the GLB on save or export.

  • Min size to compress (bytes) – Attachments smaller than this threshold are stored uncompressed.

  • Set to default – Resets attachment compression settings to factory defaults.
